kept.text:
Sir Peter Beck took $RKLB from a company once valued in the single-digit millions to a multi-billion-dollar space platform business, largely by vertically integrating propulsion, launch, and satellite infrastructure ahead of demand.

What stands out is how naturally he has executed on building the picks-and-shovels layer of the space economy rather than chasing headline missions, turning constraints into internal capabilities.

That strategy is why Rocket Lab today is positioned not just as a launch provider but as a full space systems company participating in the same structural growth themes driving the modern space infrastructure cycle.

kept.text:
My $RKLB 5/7 earnings prediction.

SPB will put on an absolute show, and backtrack a bit for newer investors.  SpaceX will drive a lot more visibility to this call.

Expect a dog and pony with the new products -the high-performance star tracker and Gauss.  He'll speak to Mynaric closing and what that enables.  He'll recap $RKLB's position in solar and emphasize the role (and big contract) of HASTE.  

Electron update.  BAU.  They're cooking.

Then Neutron (queue all the pretty pictures).  On track for Q4.  Testing updates (do we get new green tickmarks?).  There is literally NO WAY there is any reference to a delay (but as always he'll caveat space is hard, etc.).  Overall the takeaway will be IT'S COMING.  

Maybe another surprise?  

Adam Spice then speaks to financials.  Right on track with growth trajectory.  Probably increase guidance with new products.  

The narrative is what's important.  SPB knows it.  

The goal of THIS earnings is to attract NEW investors leading to the biggest IPO in history.  

I'm pumped.

tweet.text:
I assume you're looking for a stock in the space thematic that isn't trading at +10x sales (or more). 

-> $ASTS is at 117x sales 
-> $RKLB is at 52x sales

Frequency Electronics | $FEIM

No satellites, no missiles, no GPS, no military communications - it's just the invisible layer that makes all of it work. 

The only public competitor to $FEIM's atomic clocks is $MCHP which trades at 10x sales with negligible growth compared to $FEIM.

With $FEIM set to hit ~$150M in revenue by FY29, you're looking at a company growing +32% CAGR over the next 3 years. 

That's just slightly less growth than a company like $RKLB that trades at 52x sales. 

Obviously, I'm not comparing $RKLB to $FEIM. That'd be an idiotic comparison. $RKLB's TAM is infinite compared to $FEIM's niche market. 

But you do have two high growth space companies trading at VERY different multiples. 

Even given $FEIM a 50% discount to a company like $RKLB and you're looking at a $3.75B company (vs $566M today). 

Or you can think of it this way. $RBC is a precision engineering component play for robotics. Yet you have $RBC trading at 10x sales with growth rates ~1/3rd of $FEIM's whilst $FEIM trades at 7x sales. 

I think at bare minimum $FEIM deserves ~12x multiple over time given their moat in this space and their growth. 

12x on $150M = $1.8B (2.5x from today). 

This is a high growth component play in the space thematic. 

I bought before yesterday's 15% move. Admittedly lucky timing.

quoted_tweet.text:
Good morning ☕️🦀🇺🇸 Happy Saturday!

The stock market had a strong week overall for the period ending Friday, May 2, 2026 (trading days April 28–May 1), with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq posting weekly gains and new record closes, while the Dow was slightly negative.

Major Indices Performance (Week of April 28–May 1)
•S&P 500: Closed at 7,230.12 (+0.29% on Friday; weekly gain ~0.3–0.6% range across reports). It hit new all-time highs multiple times.
•Nasdaq Composite: Closed at 25,114.44 (+0.89% on Friday; stronger weekly gain). Also set new records, driven by tech.
•Dow Jones Industrial Average: Closed at 49,499.27 (-0.31% on Friday; slight weekly loss).

April as a whole was exceptional: The S&P 500 gained over 9–10% (best monthly performance since 2020), Nasdaq ~15% (best since 2020), and Dow ~7%. This reflected a sharp recovery from earlier geopolitical pressures.

Key Drivers and Themes
•Strong Corporate Earnings: A heavy earnings week boosted sentiment. Big Tech results were mixed but generally supportive:
◦Apple (AAPL) reported strong Q2 results on April 30: Revenue $111.2B (+17% YoY), EPS $2.01 (+22% YoY), beating estimates. Shares rose >3% on Friday.
◦Other highlights included Alphabet (strong results), Caterpillar (big Dow boost), and mixed Big Tech reactions around AI capex/spending concerns (e.g., Meta, Microsoft).
•Geopolitics and Oil: Ongoing U.S.-Iran tensions and Strait of Hormuz disruptions kept oil volatile and elevated (WTI near $100+/barrel at points, with spikes higher). This pressured some sectors but markets largely shrugged it off amid ceasefire hopes and resilient earnings.
•Breadth and Style: Tech and growth led (especially AI-related), while broader market participation was narrower. Value and small-caps were more mixed.
•Economic Backdrop: Resilient U.S. data (e.g., retail sales, PMIs) supported the rally despite Fed signals and inflation concerns. Markets showed optimism heading into May.

Sector and Market Notes
•Tech, communication services, and select cyclicals (e.g., industrials) outperformed.
•Energy was volatile with oil prices.
•Defensive areas lagged somewhat.
•International markets were mixed, with some developed markets underperforming U.S. indices.

Overall Sentiment: Bullish recovery mode. The market demonstrated resilience amid geopolitical noise, rewarding earnings strength and AI optimism. “Sell in May” concerns were discussed but not dominating, given recent momentum.

This recap is based on closing levels and reports as of May 1–2, 2026. Markets move fast—always check real-time sources for the latest.

tweet.text:
Rarely seen a stock as hated as $RDW right now… then again, I've seen this plenty of time before.

2023: $RKLB was "a fake SpaceX"

2024: Starlink was going to crush $ASTS

2025: $PL was uninvestable with bad management.

All these takes made sense looking back, but were clearly wrong in hindsight.

Same playbook, different tickers.

Execution takes years to show up in the numbers. If you follow closely, you can often spot the turn long before it prints.

Markets tend to judge on reported results but investing is about understanding the trajectory behind them.

Do your own homework and stay forward-looking.

Investing is a competitive sport, you make money by placing your chips before the crowd shows up.
